finding_results.notes: Three targeted changes roughen the crowd exchange. (1) Line 71: added overlap where someone lost in the binder asks 'Which section is that even in?' while the pointed question is still being pressed — partial misunderstanding amid crowd pressure. (2) Line 103: Adewale's question revised from a single polished rhetorical line to a self-correcting read-through of the clause ('This part — survives retirement. So when I'm done, when I'm forty-five coaching kids somewhere, they still have all of this?'). (3) Line 111: the gymnast's three-beat thesis ('That's not bars. That's me before bars. That's my blood.') compressed to one interrupted utterance ('that's not even — that's my blood'). The crowd now has two clear instances of roughness (overlap/partial misunderstanding at line 71, self-correction at line 103), and no more than one non-core athlete (Aissatou, whose confrontational question is already alive) delivers a clean multi-sentence issue summary. Yusuf and Petra's technical-distinction dialogue preserved unchanged.

summary: Roughened the contract-debate crowd exchange across three points: added binder confusion as overlap in the cyclist section, gave Adewale a self-correcting clause-reading instead of a polished rhetorical question, and compressed the gymnast's three-beat diagnosis into one interrupted line. The room now sounds more like a frightened group working through a contract than a panel handing thematic clauses from one spokesperson to the next. Core informational beats, Yusuf/Petra technical distinction, Kai interiority, and chapter architecture preserved.
